content: Just as high school language arts teaching uses explicit reflection practice as a graded skill to build students’ critical thinking instead of treating it as an implicit innate ability, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be formalized into a scaffolded, iterative curriculum for fine-tuning, where agents progress from basic error detection to advanced multi-perspective solution critique. This scaffolded skill-building approach draws directly from the educational framing of reflection as a learnable competency rather than a emergent afterthought.
